other_observers: Brad Biggerstaff; Bruce Baker found this bird on 11/17/2011 and reported it to COBirds
specific_location: Pine Ridge Natural Area, along east shore of Dixon Reservoir
description: Small passerine (about 5 inches) with thin, pointed bill. Short tail with white flashes when spread during takeoff for flight. Upperparts not well observed except for gray wing with faint paler gray single wingbar (tips to secondary coverts). Top of head dark yellow. Underparts mainly bright lemon-yellow with white lower belly and vent. Yellow throat and face.Behaviors: Foraging in grasses beneath trees at edge of a small lake. Flitting among low branches.Call: nonePlumage: basic, formative
